title: Dark Speculation Deep Dossiers
boundary: Speculation-only layer. These subjects are tracked as claim-history, internet motifs, public-record-adjacent questions, debunk lanes, and open-source leads. They are not presented as fact. The page does not host illegal material, identify private victims, publish doxxing, describe graphic material, or make direct accusations against real people without source-specific evidence.
sourceMethod: Separate record, allegation, rumour, symbol, edited media, online lore, and court finding.
Every subject needs: origin, core claim, evidence status, counter-source, what would change the classification, and safe next route.
High-impact claims require high-grade evidence: court record, law-enforcement record, authenticated archive, forensic chain, official document, or credible named-source reporting.
If a claim points toward illegal or exploitative material, the page tracks only the public claim-history and source debate, not the material itself.

dossiers.title: Frazzledrip claim-history / alleged media narrative
dossiers.currentStatus: Track as claim-history and debunk lane only. Do not state that the alleged material exists. Do not host, request, describe, or link to illegal or exploitative material.
dossiers.originPattern: Pizzagate/QAnon-adjacent online rumour cluster tied to hacked-email interpretation, alleged hidden-media claims, and viral forum narratives.
dossiers.whyItMatters: It shows how extreme online claims spread through secrecy, shock, coded-language interpretation, distrust of institutions, and missing source chains.
whatToTrack: how the claim redirects attention from document-based trafficking research
dossiers.boundary: No illegal material. No private victim names. No direct accusations. Treat as unverified extreme internet lore unless a lawful source-grade record appears.

dossiers.title: Pizzagate / coded-network claim cluster
dossiers.currentStatus: Treat the specific criminal narrative as debunked unless new source-grade evidence appears. Keep it as a case study in coded-language interpretation, viral network storytelling, and real-world harm from unsourced accusations.
dossiers.originPattern: 2016 hacked-email interpretation, restaurant and social-media claims, forum amplification, coded-language theories, and association mapping.
dossiers.whyItMatters: It teaches the evidence rules: coded words, symbols, photos, and association maps are not proof without source-grade records.
whatToTrack: how similar narratives attach to later Epstein releases
dossiers.boundary: Do not repeat the specific criminal allegation as fact. Classify documents, counter-sources, and source gaps.

dossiers.title: Adrenochrome motif / elite immortality rumour cycle
dossiers.currentStatus: Track as myth-history, QAnon/Pizzagate-adjacent motif, and moral-panic pattern. Do not present harvesting or abuse claims as fact.
dossiers.originPattern: Older drug and occult fiction, Satanic Panic motifs, QAnon-era viral claims, celebrity rumour loops, and recycled shock narratives.
dossiers.whyItMatters: It is one of the darkest viral motifs and can contaminate serious trafficking research if it is not separated from court-record evidence.
whatToTrack: how it merges with celebrity and trafficking claims
dossiers.boundary: Extreme claim. Track as mythology unless source-grade criminal evidence exists.

dossiers.title: Project Blue Beam / staged-sky-event narrative
dossiers.currentStatus: Classic narrative revived during drone, UAP, hologram, AI-video, and disclosure cycles. Track as scenario speculation, not a confirmed plan.
dossiers.originPattern: Serge Monast-era New World Order claim involving staged alien, religious, or mass-perception deception.
dossiers.whyItMatters: It sits at the crossing point of UAP disclosure, AI media fakery, military technology, psychological operations, religious symbolism, and crisis governance.
dossiers.boundary: Track capability and narrative use. Do not claim a staged event is planned without primary records.

dossiers.title: Control-system fusion: ID + money + speech + movement
dossiers.currentStatus: Strongest speculation lane because many components are public-record: digital ID, CBDC research, online safety law, health data systems, border biometrics, climate finance, and AI governance. The open question is integration and lock-in.
dossiers.originPattern: Agenda 2030/global standards arguments, CBDC concerns, digital identity rollout, pandemic pass memories, platform moderation, and security-state expansion.
dossiers.whyItMatters: This is the bridge between speculation and public-record tracking: individual claims remain weak until laws, pilots, contracts, standards, and mandates connect.
dossiers.boundary: Use convergence scoring. Do not claim a finished world-control system unless source-grade records show integration and lock-in.
